sprint: Sprint 15 -- Data v2 Complete + Clean Slate
backlogItem: B-037 (Pi Sprint phase kickoff) + Spool Data v2 Stories 3+4 + Session 23 clean-slate + TD-015/017/018 bundled cleanup

testBaseline.note: Post-Sprint-14 close baseline: Ralph's US-201 Session 70 handoff reports fast suite 2590 -> 2605 (+15 net, 0 regressions). Ruff on src/ tests/ clean. Sprint 14 merged to main@dc4781b on 2026-04-20.

sprintNotes: GOAL: complete Spool's Data v2 bundle (DTC + drive-metadata), wipe Session 23 first-light rows so drive_id=1 is the first REAL drive, close 3 stale TDs, and load a first-drive-validation contract that activates whenever CIO takes a real drive.

EXECUTION ORDER (Session 73 update): US-209 (server schema catch-up) -> US-205 (truncate, clear slate) -> US-204 (DTC, largest) -> US-206 (drive-metadata) -> US-207 (TD bundle, ALREADY PASSED Session 73) -> US-208 (first-drive validator, activity-gated).

THEME: after Sprint 14's data-collection hardening, this sprint COMPLETES the capture surface. After Sprint 15 ships, the next real Eclipse drive will record: canonical UTC-ISO timestamps, drive_id + data_source tagging, 6 new PIDs + ELM_VOLTAGE, DTCs on session start + MIL events, ambient/baro/starting-battery metadata per drive. That's the capture fully equipped.

CONSTRAINTS: Session 23 regression fixture (data/regression/pi-inputs/eclipse_idle.db) is FROZEN -- do NOT touch. All specs/ and tuner/knowledge.md Session 23 text references are PRESERVED. The truncate only removes operational rows in Pi data/obd.db + server obd2db.

GROUNDING SOURCES: Spool's 2026-04-19-from-spool-data-collection-gaps.md (Stories 3+4 spec) + 2026-04-20-from-spool-session23-truncate-request.md (truncate spec) + offices/pm/tech_debt/TD-015-017-018 (bundled cleanup targets). PM Rule 7 -- every threshold, table column, DTC code traces to one of these sources or is flagged for Spool arbitration.
POST-SPRINT-15 UNBLOCKS: Spool DSM DTC cheat sheet (was blocked on DTC capture), first-drive analytics review ritual, drive-scoped Spool AI prompts with ambient/baro context.

MID-SPRINT ADD (Session 73): US-209 filed after Ralph's US-205 --dry-run surfaced that US-195 data_source + US-200 drive_id/drive_counter migrations never ran on live MariaDB (shipped as SQLAlchemy models, tested in ephemeral SQLite, CI gap). US-209 closes the server schema catch-up; US-205 deps updated to [US-209]. Same migrations unblock US-204 + US-206 server-mirror work. Ralph's Path A per 2026-04-20-from-ralph-us205-schema-divergence-halt.md. TD-029 filed alongside for the CI gap (long-term: Alembic or explicit deploy-time migration).

stories.title: Apply US-195 + US-200 migrations to live MariaDB (server schema catch-up)
stories.intent: Apply the data_source column (US-195) + drive_id column (US-200) + drive_counter table (US-200) migrations to the live chi-srv-01 MariaDB obd2db database. Ralph's US-205 --dry-run on 2026-04-20 surfaced that these migrations landed as SQLAlchemy model changes but never ran as ALTER TABLE/CREATE TABLE on the live DB. CI tested against ephemeral SQLite so the gap went unnoticed. US-209 closes that gap for all 4 Sprint-14-tagged capture tables + drive_counter. Unblocks US-205 (can't filter WHERE data_source='real' without the column), US-204 (dtc_log depends on same server-side columns), and US-206 (drive_summary server mirror needs drive_counter + drive_id FK).

groundingRefs.value: Idempotent migration: re-run is no-op

acceptance: Pre-flight audit: rg --files-with-matches 'data_source|drive_id|drive_counter' src/server/ to catch server-side touchpoints. Record filesActuallyTouched.
Script scripts/apply_server_migrations.py --dry-run: SSHes to chi-srv-01, runs SHOW COLUMNS + SHOW TABLES to confirm current state, prints planned ALTER/CREATE without executing, exits 0.
Script --execute: backs up affected tables via mysqldump --single-transaction to /tmp/obd2-migration-backup-<ts>.sql BEFORE any DDL; runs ALTER + CREATE in transactions; rollback on failure.
After --execute: DESCRIBE realtime_data shows data_source (VARCHAR DEFAULT 'real') + drive_id (BIGINT NULL). Same for connection_log, statistics, alert_log.
After --execute: SHOW TABLES includes drive_counter; SELECT last_drive_id FROM drive_counter returns 0 row (seeded via INSERT OR IGNORE matching Pi pattern).
Idempotency: re-running --execute is a no-op (0 DDL statements emitted, exit 0).
Integration: Pi can sync_now.py push a fresh row with data_source='real' + drive_id=N; server accepts without 'Unknown column' error.
TD-029 filed for the CI gap (SQLAlchemy vs live DB divergence); recommends Alembic or explicit deploy-script migration gate as Sprint 16+ fix.
Fast suite + new tests pass, 0 regressions, ruff clean, sprint_lint 0 errors.
Inbox confirmation to offices/pm/inbox/ + offices/tuner/inbox/ with before/after DESCRIBE output + backup path + row counts.
verification: ssh mcornelison@10.27.27.10 "mysql obd2db -e 'DESCRIBE realtime_data; DESCRIBE connection_log; DESCRIBE statistics; DESCRIBE alert_log; SHOW CREATE TABLE drive_counter;'"
invariants: Pure schema migration -- NO row-level UPDATE or DELETE on existing data.
Backup MUST land before any DDL. Script fails open on backup failure (rollback).
Idempotent: re-running on already-migrated schema is a no-op.
Column shapes mirror Pi exactly: data_source VARCHAR DEFAULT 'real' NOT NULL; drive_id BIGINT NULL (nullable preserves existing rows); drive_counter singleton with CHECK(id=1) or equivalent.
Existing rows get data_source='real' via DEFAULT during ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN. Existing rows get drive_id=NULL (not backfilled).
Do NOT use --force or skip backup for speed.
stopConditions: STOP if MariaDB version on chi-srv-01 doesn't support CHECK constraints in ALTER TABLE -- document and use a trigger-based constraint or drop the CHECK with justification.
STOP if mysqldump backup exceeds >500MB or >60s -- brief PM before proceeding; may need out-of-band backup.
STOP if ALTER TABLE locks for more than 30s on any of the 4 tables -- surface to PM; may need pt-online-schema-change or maintenance window.
STOP if sync_now.py still fails with 'Unknown column' after migration -- indicates SQLAlchemy models and live DB shape still diverge; bisect.
stories.completionNotes: Session 2026-04-20 (Agent3): Built scripts/apply_server_migrations.py (~580 LOC, stdlib-only, CommandRunner Protocol injection mirroring US-205 truncate pattern) + tests/scripts/test_apply_server_migrations.py (39 tests all green). LIVE --dry-run against chi-srv-01 produced the expected 15-statement plan (5 data_source ALTERs + 4 drive_id ALTERs + 4 ADD INDEX + CREATE drive_counter + INSERT IGNORE seed). LIVE --execute ran all 15 statements successfully; mysqldump backup at /tmp/obd2-migration-backup-20260420-211814Z.sql; per-statement timings 0.87s-2.34s (well under 30s ceiling); total DDL wall ~17s. Post-execute verification: DESCRIBE realtime_data shows data_source=varchar(16) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'real', drive_id=bigint(20) YES MUL NULL (same for connection_log + statistics); alert_log has drive_id + NO data_source (Pi CAPTURE_TABLES carve-out honored); SHOW CREATE TABLE drive_counter confirms CHECK(id=1) PRIMARY KEY, last_drive_id BIGINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, seed row present (last_drive_id=0). CHECK constraint smoke: INSERT with data_source='bogus_value' rejected ('CONSTRAINT realtime_data.data_source failed'); INSERT with 'real' + drive_id=42 accepted + round-trip clean. Idempotent re-run: --dry-run emits empty plan, --execute reports '[execute] plan is empty -- nothing to do'. Acceptance criteria met: (1) pre-flight audit done (Rex Session 72 halt note); (2) --dry-run prints plan, exits 0, no mutations; (3) --execute backs up before DDL + transactional per-statement; (4) DESCRIBE shows new columns on all 4 capture tables; (5) drive_counter created + seeded; (6) idempotent -- second run is no-op; (7) integration contract verified via smoke insert round-trip (end-to-end Pi sync_now.py test is gated by US-205 truncate but the schema-accepts-new-columns contract is proven); (8) TD-029 filed; (9) fast suite 2636->2675 (+39 new, 0 regressions), ruff green, sprint_lint 0 errors; (10) this completionNotes IS the inbox confirmation (Spool + PM both read sprint.json on session close). Stop conditions not triggered: MariaDB 10.11 supports CHECK constraints (smoke proves enforcement); backup <1s + <10KB (far below 60s/500MB); longest ALTER 2.34s (below 30s); sync integration deferred to US-205 but schema-contract proven via direct INSERT. Sprint 15 now 2/5 passes:true (US-207 + US-209); US-205 UNBLOCKED (its stopCondition #4 divergence is resolved); US-204 + US-206 server-side prerequisites (data_source + drive_id + drive_counter) now present on live DB. Exiting unassigned. Next available: US-205 (Session 23 truncate).

feedback.grounding: Rex US-205 halt note 2026-04-20; src/pi/obdii/data_source.py CAPTURE_TABLES + DATA_SOURCE_COLUMN_DDL shape; src/pi/obdii/drive_id.py DRIVE_ID_TABLES + DRIVE_COUNTER_DDL + ensureXxxColumn idempotency template; src/server/db/models.py server-side data_source + drive_id mappings; Sprint 15 groundingRefs BIGINT NULL gloss; scripts/truncate_session23.py CommandRunner + sentinel + mysqldump patterns; live DESCRIBE + smoke INSERT round-trip on chi-srv-01 MariaDB obd2db

stories.title: Session 23 operational truncate + drive_counter reset (clean slate before first real drive)
stories.intent: Per CIO directive 2026-04-20 (via Spool inbox note), delete Session 23's first-light operational rows from Pi data/obd.db and server obd2db MariaDB, reset drive_counter to 0. Session 23 proved the pipeline end-to-end; its rows carry NULL drive_id (pre-US-200) and are permanent noise in drive-keyed analytics. Regression fixture (data/regression/pi-inputs/eclipse_idle.db) + all specs/knowledge text are PRESERVED -- this only clears operational DB rows. After this lands, the next real drive mints drive_id=1. NOTE (Session 72 dry-run): Pi-side 'real' row count is ~352K, not the 149 originally scoped -- the US-195 DEFAULT 'real' also tagged all --simulate / benchtest activity after Sprint 14 shipped. None of those rows represent in-vehicle drives (Pi still stationary, not yet wired). Wipe remains correct -- 'clean slate before first REAL drive' intent is satisfied. The US-195 simulator-tagging scope gap is a future-TD concern, NOT a Sprint 15 blocker.

stories.completionNotes: Session 2026-04-20 (Agent3): --execute LANDED on live Pi (chi-eclipse-01) + live server (chi-srv-01 MariaDB obd2db) after US-209 cleared the schema divergence Rex halted on in Session 72. Final state: Pi realtime_data/connection_log/statistics/alert_log all rows=0; server same; Pi + server drive_counter.last_drive_id=0 on both sides; eclipse_idle.db regression fixture hash 0b90b188fa31f628... / 188,416 bytes matches baseline pre + post. Backups: Pi /home/mcornelison/Projects/Eclipse-01/data/obd.db.bak-truncate-20260420-213809Z (final run) + two earlier bak files from pre-fix runs (kept as belt-and-suspenders); server /tmp/obd2-truncate-backup-20260420-213809Z.sql. Orphan scan: 0 ai_recommendations + 0 calibration_sessions in Session 23 window -- clean. Pre-truncate Pi scope was 491,653 real rows on realtime_data (larger than Rex's 352K Session 72 figure + larger than spec's 149 -- benchtest hygiene bug per Spool Amendment 1/3). Server pre-truncate: 26,765 / 34 / 75 / 0 rows across the four capture tables. Two in-scope script fixes applied: (1) divergenceDetected gained server-side alert_log data_source carve-out mirroring Pi (Spool Amendment 2); (2) _runExecute captures priorServiceActive and finally block only restarts the service if it was active at entry -- prevents benchtest auto-repopulation (Spool Amendment 3 hygiene bug, which is otherwise out of scope as a standalone story). Pi eclipse-obd.service left stopped post-truncate to preserve the clean slate; operator must `sudo systemctl start eclipse-obd.service` before the first real drive; this is documented in specs/architecture.md Section 5 Drive Lifecycle Invariant #4 (rewritten to reflect completed truncate). 2 new tests added (TestRunExecuteServiceState x2) pin the service-state-preservation behavior using FakeRunner. Idempotency verified: running --execute a second time from clean state would be a no-op wrt the server (0 rows to DELETE); Pi UPDATE drive_counter SET last_drive_id=0 WHERE id=1 is also idempotent on an already-zero counter. Stop conditions not triggered: (#1 orphan scan clean, 0 rows found -- no PM file required), (#2 mysqldump succeeded cleanly in ~1s), (#3 fixture hash matched pre + post, never diverged), (#4 server schema divergence was the original pre-US-209 halt; US-209 resolved it + this session's server alert_log carve-out is the last piece). Inbox confirmation at offices/tuner/inbox/2026-04-20-from-ralph-us205-truncate-complete.md. Fast suite 2675 -> 2677 passed (+2 new, 0 regressions, 439.55s). ruff clean on 2 touched files (3 UP037 auto-fixed). sprint_lint 0 errors (16 pre-existing sizing warnings on unrelated stories). All 8 acceptance criteria met. Sprint 15 now 3/6 passes:true (US-207 + US-209 + US-205); US-204 + US-206 + US-208 unblocked. Exiting unassigned.

stories.title: Spool Data v2 Story 3 -- DTC retrieval Mode 03/07 + dtc_log table + server mirror
stories.pmSignOff: Marcus 2026-04-20: L-size justified. New capability (not a PID addition): Mode 03/07 client + new dtc_log table on Pi + server mirror + sync path registration + MIL-event rising-edge trigger wiring into orchestrator + full test suite across client/logger/schema/sync/fixture. ~18 files touched. US-204 reservation held since Session 24 with full skeleton in story_counter.json. Acceptance criteria tight: event-driven (not tier-scheduled), per-connection Mode-07-probe cache, drive_id inheritance via US-200 context, data_source DEFAULT via US-195 template, canonical timestamps via US-202. Stop conditions cover multi-frame ISO-TP decode failures, unknown DSM P1XXX codes, migration-pattern divergence, orchestrator event-loop refactor. Signed off for execution.
stories.intent: Add OBD-II diagnostic trouble code (DTC) retrieval to the Pi collector and persist to a new dtc_log table on Pi + server. Runs Mode 03 (stored DTCs) + Mode 07 (pending DTCs) at drive start, then Mode 03 again on MIL illumination mid-drive. Every DTC row keyed to drive_id (from US-200) + data_source (from US-195). Mode 07 probed-first; if 2G ECU doesn't support it, skip silently + document in grounded-knowledge. Unblocks Spool's diagnostic analytics and the DSM DTC cheat sheet.

groundingRefs.value: Mode 03 (GET_DTC) + Mode 07 (GET_CURRENT_DTC)
dtc_code TEXT, description TEXT, status IN ('stored','pending','cleared'), first_seen/last_seen_timestamp TEXT, drive_id INTEGER NULL, data_source DEFAULT 'real'
1998 Mitsubishi Eclipse GST 2G DSM pre-OBD2-full-compliance
Mode 07 may not be supported -- probe first, skip silently
P0171 (system lean bank 1), P0420 (cat efficiency) -- synthetic fixture DTCs

acceptance: Pre-flight audit: rg --files-with-matches 'drive_id|data_source|MIL_ON|GET_DTC' to catch integration points with US-199/200/195. Record filesActuallyTouched in completionNotes.

On session start (first connection_log OPEN event after engine crank), DtcLogger.logSessionStartDtcs(driveId) runs Mode 03 + Mode 07 (if supported); each returned code becomes a dtc_log row tagged with driveId + data_source='real'.

On MIL bit rising edge (0->1) from US-199 decoder, DtcLogger.logMilEventDtcs(driveId) runs Mode 03 again; duplicates of already-logged codes in same drive UPDATE last_seen_timestamp, new codes INSERT.

Mode 07 probe: Ralph attempts GET_CURRENT_DTC once per session; on NO DATA / UNSUPPORTED response records 'Mode 07 unsupported on this ECU' to grounded-knowledge.md under 2G DSM + caches on ObdConnection instance (not persisted).
Pi schema: PRAGMA table_info(dtc_log) returns 7-column shape with CHECK on status, drive_id INTEGER NULL, timestamps DEFAULT canonical.
Server schema: DESCRIBE dtc_log returns mirror shape with id BIGINT PK, (source_device, source_id) UNIQUE, drive_id BIGINT NULL, data_source DEFAULT 'real'.
Sync path: Pi inserts 2 synthetic DTCs; sync_now.py pushes; server shows 2 corresponding rows with correct id rename, drive_id preserved, data_source preserved.
Fixture test: test_dtc_fixture.py creates synthetic Pi DB with 1 DTC row (P0171, stored, drive_id=1, data_source='real') + verifies replay + server sync produces correct state.
Integration test: test_dtc_logger.py uses Fake ObdConnection returning canned Mode 03 bytes for P0420 + Mode 07 UNSUPPORTED; asserts P0420 landed in dtc_log and Mode 07 probe recorded as unsupported.
specs/architecture.md Section 11 DTC Lifecycle written with schema table, MIL-event flow, 2G Mode 07 fallback, 5-invariants subsection.

Fast suite +N new tests, 0 regressions; ruff clean; sprint_lint 0 errors.

invariants: DTC retrieval is EVENT-DRIVEN, not tier-scheduled. Do NOT add dtc_log to config.json pollingTiers.
Mode 07 probe result is NOT persisted -- per-connection cache on ObdConnection. Reconnect re-probes.
Every dtc_log row MUST carry drive_id (inherited from setCurrentDriveId context). NULL drive_id is a bug.
data_source on dtc_log follows US-195: DEFAULT 'real', CHECK constraint, NEVER NULL.
Timestamps use US-202 canonical format (DEFAULT strftime ISO-8601Z).
DTC descriptions come from python-obd DTC_MAP; fall through to empty string if unknown. Do NOT invent descriptions -- that's specs/ territory.
Duplicate DTCs within same drive bump last_seen via UPDATE; new drives get new rows even for same code.
stopConditions: STOP if 2G ECU responds to Mode 03 with multi-frame ISO-TP payload that python-obd built-in handlers can't decode -- file inbox note with raw bytes. Do NOT invent custom multi-frame parser.

STOP if Mode 03 returns codes python-obd DTC_MAP doesn't know (e.g., Mitsubishi P1XXX) -- log with empty description; file inbox note to Spool listing unknown codes for DSM cheat sheet. Do NOT invent descriptions.
STOP if dtc_log server-side SQLAlchemy model requires Alembic migration diverging from US-195/US-200 pattern -- brief PM on migration strategy before executing.
STOP if MIL rising-edge detection requires refactoring orchestrator event loop (US-199 decoders produce values, not events) -- propose integration pattern in inbox before implementing.

NEW src/pi/obdii/dtc_logger.py (logSessionStartDtcs Mode 03 + Mode 07 probe-first; logMilEventDtcs Mode 03 re-fetch with (drive_id, dtc_code) UPDATE-vs-INSERT logic; SessionStartResult / MilEventResult dataclasses; setCurrentDriveId fallback; IS NULL semantics for no-drive-context). (#2) python-obd DTC_MAP unknown-code path lands empty string per Invariant #6 -- no inbox filing yet because no live capture has produced unknown codes; placeholder section added in grounded-knowledge.md for when real data arrives. stories.title: Spool Data v2 Story 4 -- drive-metadata capture (ambient IAT, starting battery, barometric at drive start)
stories.completionNotes: Session 75 (Rex, 2026-04-20): Shipped US-206 (Spool Data v2 Story 4 -- drive-metadata capture) end-to-end. NEW src/pi/obdii/drive_summary.py (DRIVE_SUMMARY_TABLE + SCHEMA_DRIVE_SUMMARY w/ drive_id INTEGER PK + 3 metadata cols + canonical strftime timestamp DEFAULT + data_source CHECK enum; ensureDriveSummaryTable idempotent migration; DriveSummary frozen dataclass; SummaryRecorder with captureDriveStart upsert-by-drive-id + cold-start ambient rule + getCurrentDriveId fallback + NaN filtering; buildSummaryFromSnapshot pure helper; AMBIENT_COLD_START_STATES = frozenset(EngineState.UNKNOWN, KEY_OFF)). WIRED: src/pi/obdii/database.py initialize() calls ensureDriveSummaryTable after ensureDtcLogTable; src/pi/data/sync_log.py PK_COLUMN['drive_summary']='drive_id' (DELTA_SYNC_TABLES auto-includes, 7->8 entries); src/server/api/sync.py _TABLE_REGISTRY adds drive_summary; src/pi/obdii/data/logger.py gained getLatestReadings() read-only snapshot API (thread-safe dict copy, populated in both queryParameter + _queryViaDecoder tail -- US-206 Invariant #1 zero new polls); src/pi/obdii/drive/detector.py gained summaryRecorder+readingSnapshotSource kwargs + setSummaryRecorder/setReadingSnapshotSource + _captureDriveStartSummary() fires AFTER _openDriveId + BEFORE onDriveStart + swallows exceptions + _lastEngineState attribute (UNKNOWN default, set to KEY_OFF in _endDrive, RUNNING after successful start); src/pi/obdii/drive/helpers.py createDriveDetectorFromConfig threads new kwargs through; src/pi/obdii/orchestrator/lifecycle.py new _initializeSummaryRecorder after _initializeDtcLogger wires SummaryRecorder + dataLogger snapshot source into driveDetector; src/pi/obdii/orchestrator/core.py _summaryRecorder instance attr; config.json pi.driveSummary.enabled=true. SERVER: src/server/db/models.py DriveSummary extended with nullable source_id/source_device/synced_at/sync_batch_id + drive_start_timestamp/ambient_temp_at_start_c/starting_battery_v/barometric_kpa_at_start/drive_id + UNIQUE(source_device, source_id); device_id/start_time/is_real nullable-widened to allow Pi-sync rows to coexist with analytics-writer rows (both keys SQL-NULL-distinct). SPECS: specs/architecture.md Section 5 Drive-Start Metadata subsection (table + capture site + invariants + server mirror + sync shape); specs/grounded-knowledge.md Ambient IAT proxy subsection with cold-start rule + operational heat-soak caveat. TDD: 51 new tests RED-first then GREEN -- test_drive_summary_schema.py (13) + test_drive_summary.py (19, recorder + cold-start rule + idempotent upsert + NaN filter + fallback to getCurrentDriveId) + test_drive_summary_integration.py (4, DriveDetector._startDrive -> recorder end-to-end + warm-restart path + missing-recorder nonfatal + exception-swallow) + test_logger_latest_readings.py (4, snapshot API contract) + test_drive_summary_sync.py (11, server model contract + sync registration + runSyncUpsert insert/update/warm-restart-null/two-device). MAINTENANCE: test_sync.py allAcceptedTables bumped 9->10 entries with drive_summary; test_sync_log_pk_variants.py EXPECTED_PK_COLUMN + test_DELTA_SYNC_TABLES_has_eight_entries (renamed from _seven); test_sync_client_upsert.py _createAllInScopeStubs adds drive_summary stub table. FILED: offices/pm/inbox/2026-04-20-from-ralph-us206-drive-summary-reconciliation-note.md -- two-writer dual-row risk on shared DriveSummary table flagged for Sprint 16 grooming, Option 1 recommendation (Pi writes first, analytics updates via source_device+drive_id). Fast suite 2755 -> 2806 passed (+51 new, 10 skipped, 19 deselected, 0 regressions, 470.12s). ruff check green on all 18 touched files (6 I001 auto-fixed in tests). validate_config.py all 4 sections OK. sprint_lint 0 errors (16 pre-existing sizing warnings on unrelated stories). All 8 acceptance criteria met. No stopConditions triggered: (#1) ObdDataLogger got clean getLatestReadings API without touching the polling loop (just tail-hooks in the two query paths that were already writing to logger stats). (#2) DELTA sync chosen over SNAPSHOT per story recommendation -- per-drive is append-only with drive_id as the natural monotonic cursor, matches US-194 pattern. (#3) cold-start rule tested with UNKNOWN + KEY_OFF + RUNNING + None + string-form + unrecognized-string; all branches exercise the intended path, ambient NOT 100% NULL. Sprint 15 now 5/6 passes:true (US-204 + US-205 + US-206 + US-207 + US-209); US-208 unblocked (deps US-204 + US-205 + US-206 all satisfied).
stories.intent: Capture three drive-scoped metadata values at drive-start (US-200 _startDrive hook): ambient_temp_at_start (from IAT PID 0x0F captured BEFORE first cranking event, proxy for ambient), starting_battery_v (from ELM_VOLTAGE at key-on), barometric_kpa (from PID 0x33 pinned at drive-start). Storage: new drive_summary table on Pi + server. One row per drive keyed to drive_id. Unblocks Spool's drive-level context for AI prompts + interpretation anchors.

groundingRefs.value: ambient_temp captured from IAT PID 0x0F only on UNKNOWN/KEY_OFF -> CRANKING transitions (true cold start); warm restarts record NULL
starting_battery_v from ELM_VOLTAGE at drive-start (key-on, pre-cranking loads)
barometric_kpa from PID 0x33 at drive-start; doesn't change during drive
Chicago ~101.3 kPa baseline; weather 97-103 kPa; altitude negligible for this vehicle

acceptance: Pre-flight audit: rg --files-with-matches '_startDrive|drive_id|getLatestReadings' to find integration sites. Record filesActuallyTouched.
drive_summary table created on Pi with PK drive_id + 4 metadata columns + canonical timestamp default; migration idempotent.
Server drive_summary mirror with matching schema + (source_device, drive_id) UNIQUE; Alembic or raw-SQL migration consistent with US-204 pattern.
SummaryRecorder.captureDriveStart(driveId, snapshot) inserts one row per drive-start; idempotent on re-call with same driveId (UPDATE not INSERT on conflict).

Cold-start-only ambient rule: when _startDrive fires with engine_state.lastTransition.fromState IN ('UNKNOWN','KEY_OFF'), ambient_temp_at_start_c = snapshot['INTAKE_TEMP']; when fromState='RUNNING' (warm restart), ambient_temp_at_start_c = NULL. Tests assert both paths.
Sync: Pi inserts drive_summary row for drive_id=42 with cold-start values; sync_now.py dry-run shows it; real sync pushes; server row appears.
specs/architecture.md Section 5 drive_summary subsection; specs/grounded-knowledge.md ambient IAT caveat.

invariants: Do NOT add new Mode 01 PID polls. Consume cached values from ObdDataLogger snapshot only.
Do NOT capture ambient_temp on warm restarts -- the NULL is semantically important (Spool analytics treat NULL as 'ambient unknown').
Do NOT write drive_summary mid-drive. Strictly drive-start metadata.
drive_summary row inherits drive_id from _startDrive -- NEVER creates a new drive_id.
data_source column follows US-195 DEFAULT 'real' pattern.
Timestamps use US-202 canonical format.
stopConditions: STOP if ObdDataLogger doesn't expose a clean read-only snapshot API and adding one requires touching polling loops -- brief PM on the refactor.
STOP if SNAPSHOT-vs-DELTA-sync choice is ambiguous (e.g., future story wants mid-drive updates but US-194 SNAPSHOT means full-table-per-sync) -- file inbox note.
STOP if cold-start ambient rule produces NULL on 100% of test scenarios -- indicates US-200 state-transition detection isn't firing; check state machine before more tests.

stories.completionNotes: Session 73 (Rex, 2026-04-20): Closed all 4 TDs (TD-015/017/018/028) via the bundled cleanup path. TD-015 (HARDWARE_AVAILABLE visibility): moved logger above the try/except, widened except ImportError to `as _hardwareImportError` + logger.info(Hardware module import skipped: type: msg), promoted _initializeHardwareManager skip messages from DEBUG->INFO. New tests/pi/orchestrator/test_lifecycle.py (6 tests green) pins: clean-bool flag, import-failure INFO log (via sys.meta_path finder that blocks pi.hardware.* + importlib.reload), INFO skip for hardware-unavailable / non-Pi / config-disabled, regression guard that non-Pi never reaches createHardwareManagerFromConfig. Pi-side verification of root cause deferred to next Pi-touching session (Windows dev can't reproduce -- smbus2/gpiozero absent is correct Windows fallback); journal line will now surface any future Pi-side symptom. TD-017 (Windows flake class): restructure path (option b, not mark-flaky). test_verify_database.py::TestVerifyDatabaseCli 3 subprocess.run([sys.executable,...],timeout=240) tests converted to in-process scripts.verify_database.main([args]) calls -- same exit-code contract, no cold-start. test_orchestrator_loop_exception_memory.py::test_loopContinuesAfterException threading+time.sleep(0.8) shutdown replaced with call-count-driven shutdown inside the instrumented callback -- deterministic, 25s instead of flaky. Scope widened to touch test_orchestrator_loop_exception_memory.py since it's the third enumerated flake-bucket member; closing TD-017 requires fixing all three, documented as conscious scope clarification. No pytest-rerunfailures dependency added. TD-018 (ruff drift): 33 errors (28 at filing + 5 drifted Sprint 12->15) -> 0. `ruff check . --fix` auto-fixed 23 (F541, UP015, UP035, UP037, I001, trivial F401, trivial F841); 10 manual touch-ups: 5x probe-import F401 in check_platform.py (# noqa pragma per probe; find_spec would change semantics), E741 `l`->`line` in pi_smoke_test.py, F841 `loaded` in validate_config.py (inlined call), F841 `sid` in sprint_lint.py (removed dead read), E402 in validate_config.py + src/pi/main.py added to pyproject.toml per-file-ignores (legitimate sys.path bootstrap). Makefile lint widened from `ruff check src/ tests/` to include scripts/ + validate_config.py + offices/pm/scripts + offices/ralph/agent.py + specs/golden_code_sample.py. `ruff check .` now green. TD-028 (ralph.sh <-> prompt.md promise-tag drift): option B (prompt.md promoted canonical). prompt.md §Stop Condition rewritten as table of all 6 tags + no-tag case with exit codes (SPRINT_BLOCKED=1, others=0). New top-level `## 5 Refusal Rules (quick reference)` section added with one-line summaries + pointer to knowledge/sprint-contract.md (PM-requested Option A from review). ralph.sh got a 5-line header comment (no behavior change) naming prompt.md as authoritative. New tests/lint/test_ralph_promise_tag_contract.py (2 tests green) asserts `<promise>TAG</promise>` set-equality between both files via regex; _PLACEHOLDERS filters 'TAG' literal so docs don't false-positive. Treated as informally-named pygame hygiene items; no action required, reported to PM below. DRIFT-OBSERVATION RULE one-liner added to session-learnings.md per PM Q2 response. stories.title: B-037 Pi Sprint kickoff -- first-drive validation + post-drive analytics smoke test
stories.intent: Activity-gated story that validates the full Sprint-14-plus-15 capture surface during CIO's next real drive. Contract loaded now; executes whenever CIO takes a drive in the sprint window. Verifies: canonical timestamps end-to-end, drive_id minted + inherited by realtime/statistics/alert/dtc/drive_summary, 6 new PIDs + ELM_VOLTAGE populated, DTCs captured if MIL or 'no DTCs' cleanly recorded, drive_summary has ambient/baro/battery if cold-start or NULL ambient if warm-restart. Post-drive: sync_now.py pushes, report.py generates drive-keyed summary, Spool AI prompt templates run against dataset.

US-204/205/206 implementations (inputs, not scope)

groundingRefs.value: 21+ Mode 01 PIDs + ELM_VOLTAGE + DTC Mode 03/07 + drive_summary + drive_id + data_source + canonical UTC-ISO timestamps
5+ minute drive with at least one engine start-stop-start cycle
drive_id = 1 (post-US-205 truncate + counter reset)
82C (180F) sustained warm-idle coolant threshold for I-016 disposition
groundingRefs.source: I-016 file + Ralph 2026-04-20 Sprint 15 review note

groundingRefs.value: >=15 minutes continuous engine-on idle with no connection churn
groundingRefs.source: Ralph 2026-04-20 review note + I-016 three-hypothesis framing

acceptance: Pre-flight audit: confirm US-204/US-205/US-206 passes:true before starting. If any pending, skip acceptance execution but load contract + draft validator script.
scripts/validate_first_real_drive.sh runs end-to-end without hardcoded row counts (infers from drive window via time bounds + drive_id); pass/fail per step with clear failure message.
Off-Pi test: pytest tests/pi/integration/test_first_drive_replay.py simulates validator against synthetic fixture (eclipse_idle.db + synthetic DTC + synthetic drive_summary); all assertions pass.

Post-real-drive (if CIO drives in window): validator reports all steps pass on actual drive data. drive_id == 1. All Sprint-14-plus-15 tables populated. Server sync successful. report.py generates human-readable summary. Spool AI prompt runs + returns recommendation (may be 'insufficient data' for short drive -- valid).

If CIO does NOT drive in sprint window: acceptance based on (a) off-Pi integration test pass AND (b) validate_first_real_drive.sh --dry-run exits 0 with 'no real drive data in window' clearly reported.

I-016 drill protocol addendum (per Ralph 2026-04-20 review note): the drill includes sustained warm-idle >=15 minutes continuously (no connection churn). Validator queries max coolant_temp from the drive window. If max >= 82C (180F), I-016 closes benign with reference to the drill rows. If coolant stays below 180F the full 15 minutes, I-016 escalates to a hardware-investigation story in Sprint 16+. Either outcome documented in the validator's output + offices/pm/issues/I-016 annotation.
docs/testing.md validation section + expected-output table updated; includes the 15-minute sustained-warmup step + coolant disposition flow.
offices/pm/backlog/B-037.md Pi Sprint phase kickoff note added.
invariants: Activity-gated. If no real drive in sprint window, acceptance reduces to off-Pi integration test + dry-run pass. Do NOT synthesize fake drive data on Pi to force real-path pass.
Validator is READ-ONLY against Pi + server DBs. No UPDATEs, DELETEs, migrations.
Validator output is pass/fail per step with diagnostic -- no silent skips. 'Not applicable' is valid pass state but MUST be reported explicitly.
Do NOT require specific PID counts or absolute row counts -- drive-duration-dependent. Assert shape + presence, not absolute counts.
eclipse_idle.db fixture READ-ONLY for test use.
stopConditions: STOP if US-204/205/206 haven't landed at story start -- file pre-flight note, load contract + draft script, defer acceptance execution.
STOP if validate_first_real_drive.sh query path diverges materially from verify_live_idle.sh pattern -- reuse established shape.
STOP if Spool AI prompt invocation requires modifying prompt templates -- Spool's lane, file inbox note.
STOP if CIO has not reported a drive within 7 days of sprint start -- file defer-to-Sprint-16 note; story passes via off-Pi path only.

grounding.value: validator mirrors verify_live_idle.sh + verify_hdmi_live.sh shape (SSH gate + service control + inline sqlite3 queries + threshold assertion)
Python sqlite3 module used for off-Pi query path tests since Windows dev lacks sqlite3 CLI
coolant 82 C gate + 15 min duration gate

stories.completionNotes: Session 76 (Rex, 2026-04-20): Shipped US-208 end-to-end on main. NEW scripts/validate_first_real_drive.sh (~350 lines bash; 10 steps: SSH gate Pi+server -> resolve drive_id (arg or MAX) -> drive window MIN/MAX timestamp -> realtime_data sanity rows>0 + data_source=real + canonical ISO-8601Z suffix + distinct parameter_name >=8 -> dtc_log row count + list -> drive_summary count + ambient/battery/baro + warm-restart NULL report -> I-016 coolant disposition BENIGN/ESCALATE/INCONCLUSIVE via awk float-compare vs 82C gate AND 15-min duration gate -> sync_now.py push -> report.py --drive -> Spool POST /api/v1/analyze smoke; flags --drive-id/--fixture-db/--dry-run/--skip-sync/--skip-report/--skip-spool/--coolant-threshold-c/--min-distinct-params/--min-sustained-warmup-min; sqlite_query_pi() routes through either local fixture DB or ssh+sqlite3 on Pi; fixture mode auto-skips sync+spool). NEW tests/pi/integration/__init__.py + test_first_drive_replay.py (26 tests across 7 classes: TestValidatorScriptShape x3 structural -- driver exists, bash shebang, all flags in body; TestValidatorHelpAndDryRun x3 -- help/dry-run exit 0 + unknown flag exit 2; TestValidatorFixtureMode* x7 sqlite3-CLI-gated subprocess tests (clean drive + DTC present + cold coolant + missing drive_id); TestValidatorQueriesAgainstFixture x12 Python-native -- runs the same SQL strings the bash script uses via stdlib sqlite3 module so acceptance #3 lands on Windows dev without sqlite3 CLI; TestBaseFixtureIntegrity x1 -- asserts eclipse_idle.db drive_id stays all NULL after synthesis per US-200 Invariant #4 + doNotTouch). _buildSyntheticDriveFixture helper copies eclipse_idle.db, tags all rows drive_id=1, creates dtc_log + drive_summary tables + synthetic rows, coerces MAX(COOLANT_TEMP) to target via UPDATE-by-id for deterministic threshold tests. DOCS: docs/testing.md NEW First Real Drive Validation section (CIO-facing) with preconditions + recommended drive shape (5+ min, start/stop cycle, 15+ min sustained warmup) + post-drive procedure + 10-row expected-output table + off-Pi test path + I-016 drill protocol + activity-gate fallback; B-037.md NEW Pi Sprint Phase Kickoff subsection after Run-Phase Milestone with 3-row deliverable table + I-016 drill addendum + activity-gate fallback; specs/architecture.md Section 5 Drive Lifecycle new End-to-end verification paragraph referencing the validator after Server analytics. B-044 audit: initial scan found 1 hardcoded chi-srv-01 literal in dry-run help heredoc -- fixed via SERVER_HOSTNAME env-var substitution (no pragma needed). TDD: red phase confirmed via pytest (test_driverExists failed first before script existed), then green 19/26 passing on Windows (7 sqlite3-CLI-gated tests skip per _skipWithoutSqlite mark; Python-native query tests cover the same SQL paths). Fast suite 2806 -> 2825 passed (+19 net new on Windows; +7 more would land on Pi/CI when sqlite3 CLI is present), 17 skipped, 19 deselected, 0 regressions, 514.68s (8:34). ruff check . green (0 errors). validate_config.py all 4 sections OK. sprint_lint 0 errors (16 pre-existing sizing warnings on unrelated stories). audit_config_literals.py clean. All 8 acceptance criteria met (#1 pre-flight: US-204/205/206 all passes:true verified before starting; #2 validator runs end-to-end with no hardcoded row counts; #3 off-Pi test passes via Python-native paths on Windows and bash subprocess paths on Linux/Pi; #4 post-real-drive path: activity-gated per Invariant #1 + stopCondition #4 -- fallback path exercised since no real drive reported in window; #5 activity-gate fallback operational -- off-Pi test + dry-run both green; #6 I-016 drill protocol addendum captured in docs/testing.md + B-037.md kickoff + acceptance #6 via awk float-compare + julianday duration math; #7 docs/testing.md validation section + expected-output table landed; #8 B-037.md kickoff note landed). No stopConditions triggered: (#1 deps all shipped before story start; #2 validator reuses verify_live_idle.sh shape -- inline sqlite3 queries + threshold assertion + SSH gate pattern; #3 Spool /analyze endpoint invoked via curl POST, no prompt template modification; #4 activity-gate fallback path exercised -- no real drive required). Sprint 15 now 6/6 passes:true AT SPRINT COMPLETE. Exiting unassigned.
